items.verse: وَإِلَىٰ عَادٍ أَخَاهُمْ هُودًۭا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥٓ ۖ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا مُفْتَرُونَ
items.verseEn: And to ʿAad [We sent] their brother Hūd. He said, "O my people, worship Allāh; you have no deity other than Him. You are not but inventors [of falsehood].

items.verse: ۞ وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًۭا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ هُوَ أَنشَأَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱسْتَعْمَرَكُمْ فِيهَا فَٱسْتَغْفِرُوهُ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى قَرِيبٌۭ مُّجِيبٌۭ
items.verseEn: And to Thamūd [We sent] their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said, "O my people, worship Allāh; you have no deity other than Him. He has produced you from the earth and settled you in it, so ask forgiveness of Him and then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is near and responsive."

items.verse: ۞ وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًۭا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ وَلَا تَنقُصُوا۟ ٱلْمِكْيَالَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ ۚ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكُم بِخَيْرٍۢ وَإِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍۢ مُّحِيطٍۢ
items.verseEn: And to Madyan [We sent] their brother Shuʿayb. He said, "O my people, worship Allāh; you have no deity other than Him. And do not decrease from the measure and the scale. Indeed, I see you in prosperity, but indeed, I fear for you the punishment of an all-encompassing Day.

items.verse: وَلَمَّا جَآءَتْ رُسُلُنَا لُوطًۭا سِىٓءَ بِهِمْ وَضَاقَ بِهِمْ ذَرْعًۭا وَقَالَ هَـٰذَا يَوْمٌ عَصِيبٌۭ
items.verseEn: And when Our messengers, [the angels], came to Lot, he was anguished for them and felt for them great discomfort and said, "This is a trying day."

items.verse: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنشَأَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ وَٱلْأَفْـِٔدَةَ ۚ قَلِيلًۭا مَّا تَشْكُرُونَ
items.verseEn: And it is He who produced for you hearing and vision and hearts [i.e., intellect]; little are you grateful.

items.verse: قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ بَدَأَ ٱلْخَلْقَ ۚ ثُمَّ ٱللَّهُ يُنشِئُ ٱلنَّشْأَةَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ قَدِيرٌۭ
items.verseEn: Say, [O Muḥammad], "Travel through the land and observe how He began creation. Then Allāh will produce the final creation [i.e., development]. Indeed Allāh, over all things, is competent."

items.verse: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى ذَرَأَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَإِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ
items.verseEn: And it is He who has multiplied you throughout the earth, and to Him you will be gathered.

items.verse: فَاطِرُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًۭا وَمِنَ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ أَزْوَٰجًۭا ۖ يَذْرَؤُكُمْ فِيهِ ۚ لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِۦ شَىْءٌۭ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْبَصِيرُ
items.verseEn: [He is] Creator of the heavens and the earth. He has made for you from yourselves, mates, and among the cattle, mates; He multiplies you thereby. There is nothing like unto Him, and He is the Hearing, the Seeing.

items.verse: وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِن صَلْصَـٰلٍۢ مِّنْ حَمَإٍۢ مَّسْنُونٍۢ
items.verseEn: And We did certainly create man out of clay from an altered black mud.

items.verse: وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ إِنِّى خَـٰلِقٌۢ بَشَرًۭا مِّن صَلْصَـٰلٍۢ مِّنْ حَمَإٍۢ مَّسْنُونٍۢ
items.verseEn: And [mention, O Muḥammad], when your Lord said to the angels, "I will create a human being out of clay from an altered black mud.

items.verse: قَالَ لَمْ أَكُن لِّأَسْجُدَ لِبَشَرٍ خَلَقْتَهُۥ مِن صَلْصَـٰلٍۢ مِّنْ حَمَإٍۢ مَّسْنُونٍۢ
items.verseEn: He said, "Never would I prostrate to a human whom You created out of clay from an altered black mud."

items.verse: وَٱلْجَآنَّ خَلَقْنَـٰهُ مِن قَبْلُ مِن نَّارِ ٱلسَّمُومِ
items.verseEn: And the jinn We created before from scorching fire.

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ لَمْ تَرِدْ إِلَّا مَرَّةً وَاحِدَةً
categories.nameEn: Words Used Only Once in the Quran

items.verse: ثُمَّ إِنَّ لَهُمْ عَلَيْهَا لَشَوْبًۭا مِّنْ حَمِيمٍۢ
items.verseEn: Then indeed, they will have after it a mixture of scalding water.

items.verse: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ نَصِيبًۭا مِّنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْجِبْتِ وَٱلطَّـٰغُوتِ وَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ أَهْدَىٰ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ سَبِيلًا
items.verseEn: Have you not seen those who were given a portion of the Scripture, who believe in jibt [superstition] and ṭāghūt [false objects of worship] and say about the disbelievers, "These are better guided than the believers as to the way"?
And He sent against them birds in flocks,

harf.hint: He let them loose to meet, with a barrier between.
harf.verse: 55:19 — He released the two seas, meeting [one another];

deduction.title: The Shadow of Betrayal
deduction.intro: In a city teetering on the edge of moral collapse, one soul stood apart from their righteous family. While a prophet delivered a divine warning, this individual clung to the ways of the condemned, their loyalty secretly pledged to the very people destined for ruin. Their choice would seal their fate, leaving behind a cautionary tale for all time.
clues: I lived in a city whose people committed acts of unprecedented wickedness.
My household was host to divine messengers who appeared as handsome young men.
While my spouse pleaded with our people to abandon their sins, my heart remained with them.
When the angels arrived at my home, I secretly signaled their presence to the wicked townspeople.
I was commanded to leave the city at night with my family and not to look back.
But I lingered behind, and so I was overtaken by the same punishment that befell the transgressors.

trial.label: What was the event?
options: The Parting of the Red Sea
The Trial of the Golden Calf

location.label: What was my sin?
options: Building a tower to reach the heavens
Mocking a prophet for building an ark

outcome.label: What was my fate?
options: Swallowed by the earth with great wealth
Saved and given a palace in Paradise

deduction.arabic: ضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًۭا لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱمْرَأَتَ نُوحٍۢ وَٱمْرَأَتَ لُوطٍۢ ۖ كَانَتَا تَحْتَ عَبْدَيْنِ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا صَـٰلِحَيْنِ فَخَانَتَاهُمَا فَلَمْ يُغْنِيَا عَنْهُمَا مِنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًۭٔا وَقِيلَ ٱدْخُلَا ٱلنَّارَ مَعَ ٱلدَّٰخِلِينَ
deduction.verse: Allāh presents an example of those who disbelieved: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were under two of Our righteous servants but betrayed them, so they [i.e., those prophets] did not avail them from Allāh at all, and it was said, "Enter the Fire with those who enter." (66:10)

translations: If We (had) sent down this Quran
on a mountain surely you (would) have seen it humbled
And these examples We present them
to the people so that they may give thought
scramble.hint: This verse describes the profound effect the Quran would have on one of Allah's mightiest creations.
scramble.arabic: لَوْ أَنزَلْنَا هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ عَلَىٰ جَبَلٍۢ لَّرَأَيْتَهُۥ خَـٰشِعًۭا مُّتَصَدِّعًۭا مِّنْ خَشْيَةِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَتِلْكَ ٱلْأَمْثَـٰلُ نَضْرِبُهَا لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
scramble.verseEn: If We had sent down this Qur’ān upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled and splitting from fear of Allāh. And these examples We present to the people that perhaps they will give thought.

verse.arabic_text: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُغَيِّرُ مَا بِقَوْمٍ حَتَّىٰ يُغَيِّرُوا۟ مَا بِأَنفُسِهِمْ ۗ
verse.translation: Indeed, Allah will not change the condition of a people until they change what is in themselves.

theme_question.correct: Personal responsibility and inner change are prerequisites for societal change.
options: Personal responsibility and inner change are prerequisites for societal change.
Divine decree is fixed and human effort is irrelevant.
Material wealth is the primary sign of God's favor.
Only prophets can bring about significant change in a community.

educational_notes.verse_context: This powerful statement comes within a passage discussing Allah's absolute knowledge and power. It is preceded by a mention of guardian angels who protect humans by Allah's command and record their deeds. The verse thus connects divine oversight with human accountability, establishing a crucial principle of cause and effect in the spiritual and social realms.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The central theme is the unbreakable link between inner transformation and external circumstances. It establishes a fundamental law of Allah (Sunnatullah) that the fate of a community is tied to its collective moral and spiritual state. This verse empowers individuals and societies by placing the impetus for positive change in their own hands. It refutes fatalism and encourages proactive self-reform, clarifying that divine help and blessings follow sincere human effort to improve.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Ar-Ra'd (The Thunder) is a Makkan surah that powerfully argues for the truth of Allah's oneness (Tawhid), the Quran, and the Resurrection. It contrasts truth and falsehood using vivid parables, such as fertile rain versus useless scum, and draws attention to Allah's signs in nature—like thunder, lightning, and the intricate systems of the universe. The surah challenges the deniers, comforts the believers, and emphasizes that true peace of the heart is found only in the remembrance of Allah.
